title: Testosterone Cream 1%: Needle-Free TRT Through the Skin
deck: Transdermal testosterone at 1% (10 mg/g) delivers daily androgen replacement without injections, absorption variables, transfer risk, and how it compares to cypionate.
metaDescription: Testosterone cream 1% 30g for TRT: transdermal absorption, transfer precautions, dosing 50-100mg daily, vs injectable testosterone cypionate.
bodyHtml:
Testosterone 1% cream (10 mg per gram) applied to shoulders, upper arms, or inner thighs provides needle-free androgen replacement. A typical starting dose delivers 50 to 100 mg testosterone daily (5 to 10 grams of cream), titrated to morning total testosterone and symptom response.

Absorption and variability

Transdermal delivery avoids injection peaks and troughs but shows higher inter-patient variability than cypionate. Skin thickness, application site, and adherence affect levels. Washing hands after application and covering skin until dry prevents transfer to partners and children, a serious safety issue with topical androgens.

Who chooses cream

Needle-averse patients, those with injection site reactions, or men wanting daily fine-tuning. Fertility preservation is not better with cream than injection; both suppress the HPG axis at therapeutic doses.

Monitoring

Same as injectable TRT: hematocrit, PSA, lipids, symptom review. Apply to clean dry skin; rotate sites. Compounded 30g tubes are standard telehealth packaging.

CLYR Health offers testosterone 1% cream as a men's hormone preview SKU.

sources.label: Bhasin S, et al. Testosterone therapy guideline. Endocrine Society. 2018.
Snyder PJ, et al. TRAVERSE cardiovascular safety trial. N Engl J Med. 2023.
